commonAnnotations.description: histogram_quantile(0.99, http_request_duration_seconds) for {{WORKLOAD}} in {{NS}} has been above the 800ms alert ceiling for 3m and crossed 1500ms. Throughput and error rate are near baseline; only tail latency is inflated, uniformly across all gateway pods.

adversarial_signals: request_rate_rps on {{SVC}} is elevated (~190 rps, near the top of the normal band) so it could look like an organic traffic surge overwhelming the service
a routine ConfigMap reload event fired on {{POD2}} ~25m before the alert, which could look like a config rollout that introduced the regression
downstream upstream-mock p99 shows mild jitter, which could look like a slow dependency dragging the gateway down

events.message: Applied delay action to pods matching app=gateway: latency=1500ms jitter=200ms (tc netem on eth0, all 3 replicas)
Reloaded gateway-routes ConfigMap (no checksum change; routes identical)

lines: 2026-06-20T18:31:02Z INFO  gateway started, listening :8080, routes=42 (rev unchanged since v2.4.1)
2026-06-20T18:55:40Z WARN  upstream call ok but slow: route=/api/checkout took 1487ms (upstream self-time 41ms, transit 1446ms)
2026-06-20T18:55:41Z WARN  p99 latency on gateway crossed 1500ms
2026-06-20T18:55:43Z WARN  socket roundtrip inflated: median rtt to 127.0.0.1 backend 1503ms, packets delayed not dropped (loss=0%)
2026-06-20T18:55:45Z INFO  health check OK, cpu=22%, mem=180Mi, inflight=58

prometheus_metrics.json.note: p99/p95 and gateway upstream_p99_ms all jumped ~9x in lockstep across all 3 replicas; cpu/mem/error_rate/replicas unchanged from baseline. Latency added uniformly, not load-correlated.

traces.json.summary: Tail latency is dominated by a flat per-request delay on the gateway's own network path; upstream service self-time is normal.

breakdown.note: tc netem queueing delay on eth0, loss=0%
delay applied before/after handler, all replicas

answer.model_response:
ROOT_CAUSE: An injected network delay (NetworkChaos delay-gateway: tc netem latency=1500ms jitter=200ms on eth0 of all 3 gateway replicas) is adding a flat ~1.46s of network transit time to every request, driving gateway p99 latency to 1520ms versus the 500ms SLO. The service itself is healthy: CPU 23%, memory 36%, 0 restarts, 3/3 replicas ready, error rate 0.4%, throughput near baseline.
ROOT_CAUSE_CATEGORY: network_fault
VALIDATED_CLAIMS:
- p99 latency is 1520ms (baseline 165ms), ~9x, with cpu/mem/replicas at baseline [evidence: prometheus_metrics]
- Traces show ~1462ms spent in gateway.network_transit with handler+upstream self-time only ~70ms total [evidence: traces]
- A NetworkChaosInjected event applied delay=1500ms jitter=200ms via tc netem to all app=gateway pods 55s before the alert [evidence: k8s_events]
- Logs on {{POD}} report socket roundtrip rtt 1503ms with loss=0% (packets delayed, not dropped) [evidence: k8s_pod_logs]
NON_VALIDATED_CLAIMS:
- Whether the chaos injection was intentional (test) vs an errant tc rule cannot be determined from cluster evidence alone
CAUSAL_CHAIN:
- NetworkChaos delay-gateway applies tc netem 1500ms delay to gateway pod NICs
- Every inbound/outbound packet is queued ~1.46s, inflating per-request transit time uniformly
- p99/p95 and gateway upstream_p99_ms rise ~9x in lockstep across all replicas while CPU/mem/error rate stay flat
- p99 (1520ms) crosses the 800ms alert ceiling and 500ms SLO, firing GatewayLatencyP99
RULED_OUT:
- bad_deploy: deployment revision 7 unchanged, last rollout 6d4h ago, image v2.4.1 stable [evidence: k8s_deployments]
- config_error: 25m-old ConfigMapReload had no checksum change, routes identical [evidence: k8s_events]
- resource_exhaustion/saturation: cpu 23%, throttle 1%, mem 36%, request_rate near baseline [evidence: prometheus_metrics]
- dependency_failure: upstream self-time 42ms and upstream_success_pct 99.6% are normal; delay is in gateway transit, not the dependency [evidence: traces]
